summary: Frontend test (RN Expo on web) of learn.tsx Modal → inline viewState refactor for Mentova learning modules. Login flow, module list, module detail (inline), lesson detail (inline) all verified visually via screenshots. The freeze-bug fix (removing Modals, using viewState='list'|'module'|'lesson') is working correctly on web.

ui_bugs.issue: Back button uses JSX prop `data-testid="learn-back-btn"` directly on TouchableOpacity. React Native Web only converts the `testID` prop to `data-testid` attribute. Arbitrary `data-testid` props are not forwarded to the DOM by RN's TouchableOpacity. Playwright selector `[data-testid="learn-back-btn"]` failed (Timeout 30s). The visible back arrow icon does render correctly but is not accessible by the intended testid in automation. Recommend switching to `testID="learn-back-btn"` for proper RN-Web mapping.

action_items: Replace `data-testid="learn-back-btn"` with `testID="learn-back-btn"` on the back TouchableOpacity in learn.tsx (line 275) so the attribute is emitted to the DOM by react-native-web for reliable automated testing. Functionality is unaffected; this only impacts e2e selectors.
critical_code_review_comments: learn.tsx is large (~760 lines) — consider extracting ModuleDetailView, LessonView, and QuizOverlay into separate components to reduce file size and improve readability.
openLesson does not reset completion state on entry but completeLesson navigates back via goBackFromLesson which only resets selectedLesson — selectedModule retained, OK.
Quiz uses Alert.alert which is not fully supported on web (renders as window.alert). For web parity consider a custom in-app toast/modal-like overlay (already used pattern with quizOverlay).

Hardcoded French strings remain in some places ('Félicitations', 'Continuez vos efforts', 'Leçon Terminée ✓', 'Marquer comme terminé', 'Vos Badges', 'Question Suivante', 'Voir le Résultat', 'Badge obtenu') — should be moved into i18n translations.
`useNativeDriver: true` for animations triggers a warning on web; consider conditional `useNativeDriver: Platform.OS !== 'web'`.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Tested the learn module navigation flow on mobile viewport (390x844) via web. All inline view transitions work: list → module → lesson and back. No Modal/dialog elements with aria-modal/role=dialog are visibly rendered. The back arrow icon is present and visually functional in module and lesson views; however the `data-testid` attribute on TouchableOpacity is NOT serialized to DOM (RN-Web requires `testID` prop). For future automation, use `testID` instead. Screenshots /tmp/01_initial.png … /tmp/07_back_to_list.png confirm visual correctness. Note: document.body.innerText on lesson view contains text from earlier views too — this is RN-Web rendering both views but only the latest is visible via positioning/overlay; the screenshots confirm visually the correct inline view is shown without modal overlays.
rca of the issue: Original iOS freeze RCA (from prior dev work): React Native Modal components on iOS native cause main-thread blocking when stacking content; the fix replaces them with a `viewState` state variable that conditionally renders 'list' | 'module' | 'lesson' views inline within the same SafeAreaView. Quiz uses absolute-positioned overlay (StyleSheet.absoluteFill) instead of Modal. Verified on web: no aria-modal/dialog elements created when navigating module/lesson; inline rendering works smoothly. The Modal removal is correctly implemented in /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/learn.tsx.
